prompt:
Add import/export functionality to Flux so users can back up and restore their workout data.

## Context
Flux stores workout history in localStorage under these keys:
- `basement_lab_log` (LOG_KEY in app.js:7) — object keyed by `{day}_{exerciseIndex}`, each value has: exercise, weight, difficulty, failedSet, failedRep, notes, completed, day, timestamp
- `basement_lab_state` (STATE_KEY in app.js:6) — object with globalDay and currentPhase

The constants are defined at the top of app.js. The app is vanilla JS with no build system.

## Requirements

### Export
- Add an export button in the settings/menu area of the app (there's a gear icon or settings section)
- Export should download a JSON file containing both `log` and `state` data
- Format: `{"log": {...}, "state": {...}}` — same format as the existing localStorage structure
- Filename should include the date, e.g. `flux-backup-2026-04-07.json`

### Import
- Add an import button near the export button
- Import accepts a JSON file in the same format as export
- Import should MERGE log entries into existing data (not overwrite) — if a key like `5_0` exists in both, keep the existing one (don't overwrite user's current data)
- Import should NOT overwrite the current state (globalDay/currentPhase) — only import log entries
- Show a confirmation before importing (e.g. 'Import 47 workout entries? This will merge with existing data.')
- Show success/error feedback after import

### UI
- Place the import/export buttons in the settings panel (look at how theme/mode controls are laid out in app.js and follow the same pattern)
- Style them consistently with the existing app theme system
- Both buttons should work well on mobile (this is primarily a phone app)

### Testing
- Run the existing test suite and make sure nothing breaks
- The app has Playwright e2e tests — add a test that exercises export and import round-trip if feasible

## Files to modify
- `app.js` — add import/export logic
- `style.css` — add any needed styles for the new buttons
- `index.html` — add button elements if needed (check if settings panel is in HTML or generated by JS)

## Acceptance criteria
- Export downloads a valid JSON file with all workout data
- Import loads a JSON file and merges entries into localStorage
- Existing data is never lost during import
- UI is consistent with existing app design
- All existing tests pass
